sections.heading: Accounts and report of the Fund
sections.title: Accounts and report of the Fund
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Director-General maintains proper records of the Fund's accounts, sources, and use of monies and assets, and renders periodic reports to the Board. The Fund's accounts must be audited by Board-appointed auditors within three months after the fiscal year-end, following guidelines from the Auditor General for the Federation.

requirements.text: The Director-General shall keep proper records of the accounts, sources and use of the monies and assets of the Fund
The Director-General shall render accounts to the Board from time to time
The account of the Fund shall be audited not later than 3 months after the end of the year to which it relates by auditors appointed by the Board

sections.source_quote: 15. Accounts and report of the Fund (1) The Director-General of the Agency shall keep proper records of the accounts, Accounts and sources and use of the monies and assets of the Fund and shall render accounts to the Board, from time to time.

sections.heading: Cessation of membership
sections.title: Cessation of membership
sections.summary: This section outlines conditions under which a Board member ceases office, including resignation, mental incapacity, bankruptcy, criminal convictions, physical infirmity, or the President's determination that continuing in office is not in the Agency's or public interest. A vacancy is filled by appointing a successor for the remainder of the predecessor's term, maintaining the same interest representation.

requirements.text: A member of the Board shall cease to hold office if he resigns his appointment as a member of the Board by notice, under his hand, addressed to the President
A member of the Board shall cease to hold office if he becomes unsound of mind
A member of the Board shall cease to hold office if he becomes bankrupt or makes compromise with his creditors
A member of the Board shall cease to hold office if he is convicted of a felony or of any offence involving dishonesty or corruption
A member of the Board shall cease to hold office if he becomes incapable of carrying out the functions of his office, either arising from an infirmity of the mind or of the body

A member of the Board shall cease to hold office if the President is satisfied that it is not in the interest of the Agency or of the public for the person to continue in office and notifies the member in writing to that effect

When a vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board, it shall be filled by the appointment of a successor to hold office for the remainder of the term of office of his predecessor provided that the successor shall represent the same interest as his predecessor

sections.source_quote: 4. Cessation of membership (1) Notwithstanding the provision of section 3 of this Act, a member of the Board shall cease to hold office if: (a) He resigns his appointment as a member of the Board by notice, under his hand, addressed to the President; (b) He becomes unsound of mind; (c) He becomes bankrupt or makes compromise with his creditors; (d) He is convicted of a felony or of any offence involving dishonesty or corruption; (e) He becomes incapable of carrying out the functions of his office, either arising from an infirmity of the mind or of the body; or (f) The President is satisfied that it is not in the interest of the Agency or of the public for the person to continue in office and notifies the member in writing to that effect.

sections.heading: Functions of the Agency
sections.title: Functions of the Agency
sections.summary: This section details the Agency's functions, including creating frameworks for IT planning, research, standardization, and regulation in Nigeria; providing guidelines for IT application development; developing electronic governance guidelines; standardizing IT systems; rendering advisory services; creating incentives for IT adoption; collaborating with stakeholders; determining critical IT research areas; advising on IT legislation for national security; accelerating internet penetration; and performing other necessary duties.

requirements.text: The Agency shall create a framework for the planning, research, development, standardization, application, coordination, monitoring, evaluation and regulation of Information Technology practices, activities and systems in Nigeria and all matters related thereto

The Agency shall provide guidelines to facilitate the establishment and maintenance of appropriate information technology and systems application and development in Nigeria for public and private sectors, urban-rural development, the economy and the government

The Agency shall develop guidelines for electronic governance and monitor the use of electronic data interchange and other forms of electronic communication transactions as an alternative to paper-based methods in government, commerce, education, the private and public sectors, labour, and other fields
The Agency shall develop guidelines for the networking of public and private sector establishments
The Agency shall develop guidelines for the standardization and certification of Information Technology Escrow Source Code and Object Code Domiciliation, Application and Delivery Systems in Nigeria
The Agency shall render advisory services in all information technology matters to the public and private sectors
The Agency shall create incentives to promote the use of information technology in all spheres of life in Nigeria including the setting up of information technology parks

The Agency shall create incentives to promote the use of information technology in all spheres of life in Nigeria including the development of guidelines for setting up of information technology systems and knowledge parks
The Agency shall introduce appropriate regulatory policies and incentives to encourage private sector investment in the information technology industry

The Agency shall collaborate with any local or state Government, company, firm, or person in any activity which in the opinion of the Agency is intended to facilitate the attainment of the objective of this Act
The Agency shall determine critical areas in Information Technology requiring research intervention and development in those areas

The Agency shall advise the Government on ways of promoting the development of information technology in Nigeria including introducing appropriate information technology legislation, to enhance national security and vibrancy of the industry
The Agency shall accelerate internet and intranet penetration in Nigeria and promote sound Internet Governance by giving effect to the Second Schedule of this Act
The Agency shall perform such other duties, which in the opinion of the Agency are necessary or expedient to ensure the efficient performance of the functions of the Agency under this Act

sections.source_quote: 6. Functions of the Agency The Agency shall: (a) Create a framework for the planning, research, development, standardization, application, coordination, monitoring, evaluation and regulation of Information Technology practices, activities and systems in Nigeria and all matters related thereto and for that purpose, and which without detracting from the generality of the foregoing shall include providing universal access for Information Technology and systems penetration including rural, urban and under-served areas; (b) Provide guidelines to facilitate the establishment and maintenance of appropriate for information technology and systems application and development in Nigeria for public and private sectors, urban-rural development, the economy and the government; (c) Develop guidelines for electronic governance and monitor the use of electronic data interchange and other forms of electronic communication transactions as an alternative to paper-based methods in government, commerce, education, the private and public sectors, labour, and other fields, where the use of electronic communication may improve the exchange of data and information; (d) Develop guidelines for the networking of public and private sector establishment; (e) Develop guidelines for the standardization and certification of Information Technology Escrow Source Code and Object Code Domiciliation, Application and Delivery Systems in Nigeria; (f) Render advisory services in all information technology matters to the public and private sectors; (g) Create incentives to promote the use of information technology in all spheres of life in Nigeria including the setting up of information technology parks; (h) Create incentives to promote the use of information technology in all spheres of life in Nigeria including the development of guidelines for setting up of information technology systems and knowledge parks; (i) Introduce appropriate regulatory policies and incentives to encourage private sector investment in the information technology industry; (j) Collaborate with any local or state Government, company, firm, or person in any activity, which in the opinion of the agency is intended to facilitate the attainment of the objective of this act; (k) Determine critical areas in Information Technology requiring research intervention and Development in those areas; (l) Advice the Government on ways of promoting the development of information technology in Nigeria including introducing appropriate information technology legislation, to enhance national security and vibrancy of the industry; (m) Accelerate internet and intranet penetration in Nigeria and promote sound Internet Governance by giving effect to the Second Schedule of this Act; and (n) Perform such other duties, which in the opinion of the Agency are necessary or expedient to ensure the efficient performance of the functions of the Agency under this act.

sections.heading: Director General, Secretary and other staff of the Agency
sections.title: Director General, Secretary and other staff of the Agency
sections.summary: This section specifies the appointment, term of office, and responsibilities of the Director General, including being appointed by the President, serving as chief executive and accounting officer, executing policy, and managing day-to-day administration. It also outlines the Agency's authority to appoint staff, determine terms and conditions of service, and collaborate with the National Income and Wages Commission for remuneration reviews.

requirements.text: There shall be for the Agency a Director General who shall be appointed by the President
The Director General shall be the chief executive and accounting officer of the Agency
The Director General shall be responsible for the execution of policy and the day to day administration of the affairs of the Agency
The Director General shall hold office for a term of 4 years in the first instance and may be reappointed for a further term of 4 years and no more
The Agency may subject to the approval of the Board, appoint such other staff as it may deem necessary and expedient for the proper and efficient performance of the function of the Agency

The terms and conditions of service including remuneration, allowances, benefits and pensions of the staff and employees of the Agency shall be determined by the Board in such a manner as to attract and retain quality and high calibre manpower
The Board shall consider and in consultation with the National Income and Wages Commission determine and review from time to time the remunerations and allowances payable to the Agency's staff

sections.source_quote: 8. Director General, Secretary and other staff of the Agency (1) There shall be for the Agency a Director General who shall: (a) Be appointed by the President; (b) Be the chief executive and accounting officer of the Agency; (c) Be responsible for the execution of policy and the day to day administration of the affairs of the Agency; and (d) Perform such other duties as the Board may, from time to time, assign to him. (2) The Director General shall hold office for a term of 4 years in the first instance and may be reappointed for a further term of 4 years and no more and on such terms and conditions as may be specified in his letter of appointment. (3) The Agency may subject to the approval of the Board, appoint such other staff as it may deem necessary and expedient, from time to time, for the proper and efficient performance of the function of the Agency. (4) The terms and conditions of service including remuneration, allowances, benefits and pensions of the staff and employees of the Agency shall be determined by the Board in such a manner as to attract and retain quality and high calibre manpower. (5) The Board shall consider and in consultation with the National Income and Wages Commission determine and review from time to time the remunerations and allowances payable to the Agency's staff.

sections.heading: Accounts and report of the Fund
sections.title: Accounts and report of the Fund
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Director-General maintains proper records of the Fund's accounts, sources, and use of monies and assets, and renders periodic reports to the Board. The Fund's accounts must be audited by Board-appointed auditors within three months after the fiscal year-end, following guidelines from the Auditor General for the Federation.

requirements.text: The Director-General shall keep proper records of the accounts, sources and use of the monies and assets of the Fund
The Director-General shall render accounts to the Board from time to time
The account of the Fund shall be audited not later than 3 months after the end of the year to which it relates by auditors appointed by the Board

sections.source_quote: 15. Accounts and report of the Fund (1) The Director-General of the Agency shall keep proper records of the accounts, Accounts and sources and use of the monies and assets of the Fund and shall render accounts to the Board, from time to time.

sections.heading: Federal Inland Revenue Service to collect levy and pay into the Fund
sections.title: Federal Inland Revenue Service to collect levy and pay into the Fund
sections.summary: This section mandates the Federal Inland Revenue Service to assess and collect the levy imposed under section 12, pay it into the Fund, and enforce payment within 60 days. Non-payment triggers a 2% penalty, and failure to pay within two months after a demand note results in a fine of at least N1,000,000 and liability for the company's chief executive.

requirements.text: The Federal Inland Revenue Service shall assess and collect the levy imposed under section 12 of this Act

The Federal Inland Revenue Service shall while assessing any company for either company or income tax or Petroleum profit tax for an accounting period of the company, also assess such company for the levy or tax due under this Act
The levy imposed under section 12 of this Act shall be due and payable within 60 days after the Federal Inland Revenue Service has served notice to the assessment on a company

Where a levy due under section 12 of this Act is not paid within the time specified in that section, the Federal Inland Revenue Service shall serve on the company, a demand note for the unpaid tax plus a sum which is equal to 2 percent of the levy

Any company, agency or organisation that fails within two months after a demand note, to pay the levy or the import duty imposed under section 11 of this Act commits an offence and is liable on conviction to a fine of not less than N 1,000,000.00

The Chief Executive Office of the company, Agency or Organisation shall be liable to be prosecuted and punished for the offence in like manner as if he had himself committed the offence, unless he proves that the act or omission constituting the offence took place without his knowledge, consent or connivance

sections.source_quote: 16. Federal Inland Revenue Service to collect levy and pay into the Fund (1) The Federal Inland Revenue Service shall assess and collect the levy imposed under section 12 of this Act. (2) The Federal Inland Revenue Service shall while assessing any company for either company or income tax or Petroleum profit tax for an accounting period of the company, also assess such company for the levy or tax due under this Act. (3) The levy imposed under section 12 of this Act shall be due and payable within 60 days after the Federal Inland Revenue Service has served notice to the assessment on a company in such form as the Federal Inland Revenue Service may, from time to time, determine. (4) Where a levy due under section 12 of this Act is not paid within the time specified in that section, the Federal Inland Revenue Service shall serve on the company, a demand note for the unpaid tax plus a sum which is equal to 2 percent of the levy. (5) Any company, agency or organisation that fails within two months after a demand note, to pay the levy or the import duty imposed under section 11 of this Act commits an offence and is liable on conviction to a fine of not less than N 1,000,000.00 and the Chief Executive Office of the company, Agency or Organisation shall be liable to be prosecuted and punished for the offence in like manner as if he had himself committed the offence, unless he proves that the act or omission constituting the offence took place without his knowledge, consent or connivance. (6) The institution of proceeding or imposition of a penalty under this Act shall not relieve a company or Organisation from liability to pay the Federal Inland Revenue Services such levy or levies that may become due under this Act.

sections.heading: Offences
sections.title: Offences for non-compliance with NITDA Act
sections.summary: Any person or corporation that violates or fails to comply with the Act commits an offence. A body corporate that fails to pay a levy within two months after a demand note is also guilty. Officers acting on behalf of a body corporate who commit offences may be liable unless they prove they were unaware of the offence. Failure to comply with NITDA guidelines and standards also constitutes an offence. The Agency collaborates with the Standards Organisation of Nigeria to enforce these standards.

requirements.text: Any person or corporation that violates or fails to comply with the Act commits an offence
A body corporate that fails to pay a levy within two months after a demand note commits an offence
Every CEO or officer acting in that capacity commits an offence unless they prove they were unaware
Failure to comply with NITDA guidelines and standards constitutes an offence

sections.source_quote: 17. Offences (1) Except as otherwise provided in this Act, any person or corporation who contravenes or fails to comply with the provisions of this Act commits an offence.

sections.heading: Accounts of the Agency
sections.title: Agency accounts and funding sources
sections.summary: The Agency must establish and maintain an account for all subventions, budgetary allocations, gifts, loans, grants, internally generated revenues, and other sums accruing to it. This account is used to manage the Agency's financial resources and ensure proper financial management.

requirements.text: The Agency shall establish and maintain an account for all subventions and budgetary allocations from the Federal Government
The Agency shall establish and maintain an account for gifts, loans, grants-in-aid from national, bilateral and multilateral organisations and agencies
The Agency shall establish and maintain an account for rents, fees and other internally generated revenues from services provided by the Agency
The Agency shall establish and maintain an account for all other sums accruing to the Agency from time to time

sections.source_quote: 20. Accounts of the Agency The Agency shall establish and maintain an account into which shall be paid and credited: (a) All subventions and budgetary allocations from the Federal Government; (b) Gifts, loans, grants-in-aid from national, bilateral and multilateral organisations and agencies; Rents, fees and other internally generated revenues from services provided by the Agency; and (c) All other sums accruing to the Agency from time to time.

sections.heading: Expenditure of the Board
sections.title: Board expenditure and fund usage
sections.summary: The Agency may use funds from the Fund established under section 12 for administration costs, board member emoluments, staff salaries, property maintenance, financial reserves, and other expenditures related to its functions. Proceeds from the Fund must be remitted to the Federal Treasury.

requirements.text: The Agency may apply the proceeds of the Fund for the cost of administration of the Agency
The Agency may apply the proceeds of the Fund for the payment of emoluments, allowances and benefits of board members
The Agency may apply the proceeds of the Fund for reimbursing board members or committee expenses
The Agency may apply the proceeds of the Fund for the payment of staff salaries, fees, allowances, gratuities and pensions
No payment may be made to anyone receiving government emoluments
The Agency may apply the proceeds of the Fund for the development and maintenance of property
The Agency may apply the proceeds of the Fund for maintaining general financial reserves
Proceeds from the Fund shall be remitted to the Federal Treasury

sections.source_quote: 21. Expenditure of the Board The Agency may, from time to time, apply the proceeds of the Fund established under section 12 of this Act; (a) To the cost of administration of the Agency; (b) To the payment of the emoluments, allowances and benefits of members of the Board and for reimbursing members of the Board or of any committee set up by the Board and for such expenses as may be expressly authorized by the Boards; (c) To the payment of the salaries, fees or other remuneration or allowances gratuity and pensions, and other benefits payable to the staff and other employees of the Agency, so however that no payment of any kind under this paragraph (except such as may be expressly authorized by the Board) shall be made to any person who is in receipt of emoluments from the Government of the Federation or of a State; (d) For the development and maintenance of any property vested in or owned by the Agency; (e) For maintaining general financial reserves subject to general or special directive that may be given in that behalf by the Minister in accordance with the provisions of this Act; and (f) To any other expenditure in connection with all or any of its functions under this Act. (3) Proceeds from the Funds established under Section 12 (c) shall be remitted to the Treasury of the Federal Government.

sections.heading: Annual Estimates and Accounts
sections.title: Annual financial reporting and audits
sections.summary: The Agency must submit an annual estimate of its expenditure and income by September 30th each year. It must keep proper accounts and records and have them audited by auditors appointed by the Auditor General within six months after the year ends.

requirements.text: The Agency shall submit an estimate of its expenditure and income by September 30th each year
The Agency shall keep proper accounts for each year
The Agency shall keep proper records in relation to its accounts
The Agency shall cause its accounts to be audited by auditors appointed from the list in accordance with the guidelines supplied by the Auditor General within six months after the end of each year

sections.source_quote: 22. Annual Estimates and Accounts (1) The Agency shall, not later than 30th September in each year submit to the Minister an estimate of its expenditure and income (including payments to the Agency of the fund for next succeeding year); (2) The Agency shall keep proper accounts in respect of each year ad proper records in relation to those accounts and shall cause its accounts to be audited within six months after the end of each year by auditors appointed from the list in accordance with the guidelines supplied by the Auditor General for the Federation.

sections.heading: Annual Reports
sections.title: Annual reports and audited accounts
sections.summary: The Agency must prepare and submit an annual report by June 30th each year, including audited accounts and the auditor's report for the previous year.

requirements.text: The Agency shall prepare and submit an annual report by June 30th each year
The report must include audited accounts for the year
The report must include the auditor's report on the accounts

sections.source_quote: 23. Annual Reports The Agency shall prepare and submit to the Minister not later than 30th June in each year a report in such form as prescribed in this Act on the activities of the Agency during the immediately preceding year, and shall include in the report a copy of the audited accounts of the Agency for that year and of the auditor's report thereon.

sections.heading: Exemption from tax
sections.title: Tax exemption for the Agency
sections.summary: The Agency is exempt from income tax on investments made by the Board or otherwise. Tax provisions for companies or trust funds do not apply to the Agency or the Board.

requirements.text: The Agency shall be exempted from income tax on any income accruing from investments made by the Board
Tax provisions for companies or trust funds do not apply to the Agency or the Board

sections.source_quote: 26. Exemption from tax (1) The Agency shall be exempted from the payment of income tax on any income accruing from investments made by the Board or otherwise howsoever (2) The provision of any enactment relating to the taxation of companies or trust funds shall not apply to the Agency or the Board.

sections.heading: Transitional provisions
sections.title: Transitional provisions for agency succession
sections.summary: This section ensures continuity of existing contracts, personnel, records, and property from the former National Information Technology Development Agency to the new entity under the Act. It specifies that all current agreements, personnel allocations, records, and equipment automatically transfer to the successor agency without interruption. The section also allows the use of existing forms, licenses, and letterheads until supplies are exhausted.

requirements.text: All existing contracts agreements and compacts currently in effect by the National Information Technology Development Agency continue in effect.
All existing contracts currently in effect by the National Information Technology Development Agency continue in effect.

Any position authorized and allocated subject to the personnel laws of the former National Information Technology Development Agency are transferred to the National Information Technology Development Agency.

All records, property and equipment previously belonging to or allocated for use of the former National Information Technology Development Agency become, on the effective date of this Act, part of the Property of the National Information Technology Development Agency.

All existing forms, licenses, letterheads and similar items bearing the name of or referring to the "National Information Technology Development Agency" may be utilized by the National Information Technology Development Agency until existing supplies of those items are exhausted.

sections.source_quote: 33. Transitional provisions (1) The National Information Technology Development Agency is the successor-in-title in every way to the power, duties and functions of the former National Information Technology Development Agency

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.title: Definitions for the National Information Technology Development Agency Act
sections.summary: This section provides precise definitions for key terms used throughout the Act, including 'Agency', 'Board', 'Computer', 'Computer Network', 'Computer system', 'Data', 'Director General', 'Electronic form', 'Electronic record', 'Government', 'Information Technology', 'Minister', 'President', and 'Software'. These definitions establish the legal framework for interpreting the Act's provisions and ensure clarity in the agency's operations.

requirements.text: Agency means the National Information Technology Development Agency established under Section 1 of this Act.
Board means Governing Board of the Agency established under this Act.

Computer means any electronic device of computational machinery using programmed instructions which has one or more of the capabilities of storage, retrieval, memory, logic, arithmetic or communication and includes all input, output, processing, storage, software, or communication facilities which are connected or related to such a device in a system or network or control function by the manipulation of signals, including electronic, magnetic or optical.
Computer Network means the interconnection of one or more computers through;

Computer system means a device or collection of devices including input and output support devices and excluding calculators which are not programmable and capable of being used in conjunction with external files, which contain computer programmes, electronic instructions, input data and output data, that performs logic arithmetic, data storage and retrieval, communication control and other functions;

Data means a representation of information, knowledge, facts, concepts or instructions, which are being prepared or have been prepared in a formalised manner, and is intended to be processed, is being processed or has been processed in a computer system or computer network, and may be in any form (including computer printout, magnetic or optical storage media, punched cards, punched tapes) or stored internally in the memory of the computers;
Director General means the Director General of the Agency appointed under Section 8 of this Act;

Electronic form with reference to information means any information generated, sent, received or stored in media magnetic, optical, computer memory, micro film, computer generated, micro fiche or similar device;
Electronic record means data, record or data generated, image or sound stored, received or sent in an electronic form or micro film or computer generated micro fiche;
Government means the Federal Government of Nigeria;

Information Technology encompasses all forms of technology used to create, store, exchange and use information in its various forms (business data, voice, conversation, still images, motion pictures, multimedia presentations and other forms including those not yet conceived);
Minister means the Minister charged with the responsibility of matters relating to science and technology;
President means the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria;
Software includes any program, procedure and associated documentation concerned with the operation of a computer system.

sections.source_quote: 34. Interpretation In this Act: "Agency" means the National Information Technology Development Agency established under Section 1 of this Act.

sections.heading: THE NATIONAL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEVELOPMENT AGENCY ACT 2007
sections.title: Supplementary provisions for the National Information Technology Development Agency
sections.summary: This section outlines supplementary provisions for the Board of the Agency, including standing orders, meeting procedures, quorum requirements, committee appointments, and the authority to act on behalf of the Agency. It also covers the supervision of Nigeria's country code top-level domain (.ng) management, including approval of organizational constitutions, setting standards, and ensuring compliance with international best practices.

requirements.text: The Board may make standing orders regulating its proceedings or that of any of its committees.
At every meeting the Board, the Chairman shall preside and in his absence the members present at the meeting shall appoint one of their numbers to preside at the meeting.
The quorum at a meeting of the Board shall be not less than one-third of the total number of the Board members at the date of the meeting.

The Board shall for the purpose of this Act, meet not less than four times each year and subject, thereto, the Board shall meet whenever it is summoned by the Chairman, and if require to do so by notice given to him by not less than 1/3 of members, he shall summon a meeting of the board to be held within fourteen days from the date in which the notice is given.

When the Board desires to obtain the advice of any person on any particular matter, the board may co-opt him to the board for such period as it thinks fit, but a person who is a member by virtue of this paragraph shall not be permitted to vote at any meeting of the board and shall not count towards a quorum.
Subject to its standing orders, the board may appoint such number of standing or ad hoc committees as it thinks fit to consider and report on any matter with which the Board is concerned.

A Committee appointed under this paragraph shall: consist of such number of persons (not necessarily members of the Board) and a person, other than a member of the Board, shall hold office on the committee in accordance with the terms of his appointment; and be presided over by a member of the Board.
The quorum of any committee set up by the Board shall be its quorum as provided under Section 3 of this Schedule.
The decision of any committee of the Board shall constitute a recommendation to the Board.

The fixing of the Seal of the Agency shall be authenticated by the signature of the Chairman or any other person generally or specifically authorised by the Board to act for that purpose and that of the Director General.

Any contract of instrument which if made by a person not being a body corporate, would not be required to be under seal may be made or executed in behalf of the Board by the Director General or any other person generally or specifically authorized by the Board to act for that purpose.

Any document purporting to be contract, instrument or document duly signed or sealed on behalf of the Agency shall be received in evidence and shall, unless the contrary is proved, be presumed without further proof to have been signed or sealed.

The validity of any proceedings of the Board or any of its committees shall not be affected by any vacancy in the membership of the Board, or committee; any defect in the appointment of a member of the Board of committee; or reason that any person not entitled to do so took part in the proceedings of the Board or committee.

A member of the Board of a committee who has a personal interest in any contract or arrangement entered into or proposed to be considered by the Board or committee shall forthwith disclose his interest to the Board or committee and shall not vote on any question relating to the contract or arrangement.
No member of the Board shall be personally liable for any act or omission done or made in good faith while engaged in the business of the Board.
The Agency shall advise the Federal Government generally on matters and issues that are related to the management and administration of Nigeria's country code top-level domain (.ng).

The Agency shall have supervisory authority over any organization incorporated under the laws of Nigeria to manage and administer Nigeria's country code top level domain (.ng) including but not limited to the following: approve the constitution of the management of any such organization created to carry out acts mentioned in Section 2 (1) of this Schedule.

The Agency shall lay down standards which shall ensure that the membership of the organization when viewed collectively is broadly representative of the stakeholders of the ICT community in the country.

The Agency shall outline an operational rule for the organization which shall include but not limited to the following: the creation of any departments of the organization to perform specialized functions; the establishment and functioning of committees including a management board; the preparation by the organization of an annual business plan in terms of which the activities of the organization are planned annually; the determination through arbitration of any dispute concerning the interpretation of the memorandum and articles of association of the organization; the procedures and criteria for the establishment of second level domains and for delegations to such domains; the domain name dispute resolution and related appeal mechanism; criteria for the qualification of an appointment of Domain Name Hosts.
The Agency shall ensure that the activities of the organization comply with international best practice in the administration of country code top level domains.

sections.source_quote: 2007. THE NATIONAL INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY DEVELOPMENT AGENCY ACT 2007
